qa_pairs.question: How can users ensure their jobs are not wasting resources on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should ensure their jobs either use all available cores, exhaust most of the memory, or efficiently use GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: What data from Niagara was automatically migrated to Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Any data present on home, scratch, and project directories on Niagara on July 31st, 2025, that was not changed afterwards, was automatically copied to Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: What should users do if they added or modified data on Niagara after July 31st, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Data added or modified on Niagara after July 31st, 2025, may need to be copied over by the user.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user manually copy data from Niagara to Trillium after the automatic migration date?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can SSH to `nia-datamover1.scinet.utoronto.ca` and use the `cp` command to copy files locally between the mounted Niagara and Trillium file systems.

qa_pairs.question: What are the default storage quotas for home and scratch directories on Trillium as of November 4, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: As of November 4, 2025, the default storage quotas are 100 GB for home directories and 25 TB for scratch directories.

qa_pairs.question: How has the directory layout changed for home and scratch directories on Trillium compared to Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: On Trillium, home and scratch directories are no longer nested under a group; they are now located at `/home/USERNAME` and `/scratch/USERNAME`, respectively.

qa_pairs.question: Will the scratch quota and purging settings on Trillium remain the same indefinitely?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the scratch quota and purging settings will be reviewed in the coming months.

qa_pairs.question: Where can a user find their group's project directory on Trillium if they had one on Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: If a group had a project directory on Niagara, it will now be found at `/project/RRG-NAME` on Trillium, where RRG-NAME is the name of the rrg allocation.

qa_pairs.question: Do all groups on Trillium have a default project directory, and if so, what is its quota?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, all groups now have a project directory at `/project/def-PINAME` with a quota of 1 TB.

qa_pairs.question: Which Trillium file systems are backed up?
qa_pairs.answer: Home and project file systems are backed up, but scratch is not.

qa_pairs.question: How can I easily find my scratch and project directories on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: You can run the `trisetup` command once after logging in to create a `links` directory in your `$HOME` with symbolic links to your scratch and project directories.

qa_pairs.question: What other actions does the `trisetup` command perform besides creating directory links?
qa_pairs.answer: The `trisetup` command also updates `.bashrc` and other startup files, saving earlier versions.

qa_pairs.question: Will the project directory links update automatically if a user's group affiliations change?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, once set up, the links in `$HOME/links/project` will automatically update if you join, leave, or move groups.

qa_pairs.question: How do file and directory permissions in home directories on Trillium differ from Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: On Trillium, files and directories in home have the group-ownership of your own private group, unlike Niagara where the PI's group was the group-ownership.

qa_pairs.question: What steps are required to share files in your home directory with other group members on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: To share files in your home directory on Trillium, you will need to set permissions using ACLs or change the group ownership of the files.

qa_pairs.question: How are scratch directory permissions set for new users on Trillium, and how can they share files?
qa_pairs.answer: For new users on Trillium, scratch directories are private with group-ownership set to their private group; sharing files requires using ACLs or changing group ownerships.

qa_pairs.question: Do transferred scratch files from Niagara retain their original group ownership on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, users whose scratch files were transferred from Niagara will see them retain the group-ownership of the research group under which they were originally nested.

qa_pairs.question: Are project directories on Trillium configured for sharing by default?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, project directories on Trillium are set up for sharing and have group-membership corresponding to their def or rrg allocation.

qa_pairs.question: Can compute jobs write to home or project directories on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: No, home and project file systems are read-only on compute nodes on Trillium, so compute jobs must write to the scratch directory.

qa_pairs.question: When should data be copied from scratch to project or home directories?
qa_pairs.answer: Data should only be copied from scratch to project or home directories if it truly needs to be conserved and backed up.

qa_pairs.question: What methods can be used to transfer data from scratch to project or home directories?
qa_pairs.answer: Data can be transferred from scratch to project or home directories using `scp` or `rsync` commands to a datamover or login node, or by logging directly into a datamover node to perform the transfer.

qa_pairs.question: Will the NiaEnv or MistEnv software stacks be available on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the default NiaEnv software stack from Niagara and the MistEnv software stack from Mist will not be available on Trillium or Trillium-gpu, respectively.

qa_pairs.question: Which software stack is used on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium uses the CVMFS software stack, which is also available on other national clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Which modules are loaded by default on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Only the `gentoo/2023` and `CCconfig` modules are loaded by default on Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user load the standard set of default modules available on other national clusters on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can load the `StdEnv/2023` module by running `$ module load StdEnv/2023` to get the same set of default modules as on other national clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What compiler is loaded as default when `StdEnv/2023` is loaded?
qa_pairs.answer: Loading `StdEnv/2023` will load the `gcc` compiler as the default, along with many other modules.
